purpose: Frozen snapshot of Phase 5's final tournament-tuned generator state, captured before the Phase 6.5 framework_spec rewrite (RAA-3 + RAA-6). If the new generator regresses on a CVE shape Phase 5 covered, this is the reference for what 'used to work'.

validation_gates_in_order: patch_pre_match (rule fires on parent SHA blob)
patch_post_clean (rule does NOT fire on fix SHA blob)

retry_config.retry_strategy: all-validation-failure (5g) — on any non-validated outcome rebuild prompt with concrete diagnosis (rule + fixtures + match counts + diagnosis class) and retry. Schema-only retry was 5f.

recall_results.corpus: 100-CVE stratified across ecosystems (npm, pypi, maven, gomod, rubygems, packagist, cargo, nuget). 12 dropped during dry-run cache prefetch; 88 final.
notes: Per-shape: well over 80% on taint-mode CVEs with clear req.* sources; weakest on options-bag-shape (jwt.verify-style) and library-internal classes.

AI Semgrep one-shot ceiling memo (memory: ai_rule_generation_ceiling) called ~15-20% the floor for naive prompts; 5g rich-feedback retry pushed Qwen to 72.7% which roughly matches the autogrep paper's 50-60% one-shot + 20-30% retry-driven lift.
Phase 5h patch-symbols hint added concrete identifier list to the revision prompt — measurable lift on too-narrow failures (pre=0).
Forensic note: Gemini 2.5 Flash scored ~64% on the same corpus before tournament tuning; Qwen 3.5 Coder hit a ceiling around 35% one-shot before 5f-5h.

kept: callProviderAndParse() multi-provider plumbing — Anthropic prefill, OpenAI response_format json_object, Gemini systemInstruction routing.
Rate-limit retry helper (withRateLimitRetry) — 429/5xx-aware exponential backoff with retry-after honoring.
Per-CVE retry framework with rich diagnosis (5g) — but feedback now describes FrameworkSpec failures (zod issue paths + engine round-trip mismatches) instead of Semgrep grammar/match errors.
Patch-symbol hint extraction (extractPatchAddedSymbols) — generic enough to apply to any output schema.
Few-shot loader pattern — rewritten with hand-ported FrameworkSpec examples in few-shot-examples.ts.
removed: rule_yaml output field (replaced with framework_spec JSON).
Semgrep --validate gate (Gate 1 is now zod-strict; Gate 2 is engine round-trip).
Semgrep YAML grammar hints (focus-metavariable nesting, pattern-not list rejection, options-bag absence-of-key idiom, YAML-quote escaping). All Semgrep-specific.
VulnClass-playbook entries that taught the model Semgrep mode selection (mode: taint vs mode: search). FrameworkSpec has no mode discriminator — sinks are uniformly callee-pattern + argument_indices.
explicitly_at_risk: Recall on options-bag-shape CVEs (jwt.verify with missing 'algorithms' key): Phase 5 expressed these in mode: search with metavariable-pattern. FrameworkSpec callee-text matching can't distinguish jwt.verify(t,k,opts) with vs without a specific options key — these CVEs may need a separate config-default detector or a per-CVE callable check (M5+).

Recall on insecure-default CVEs (yaml.load without Loader=, requests.get(verify=False)): same shape problem. The FrameworkSpec sink pattern matches the call by name; the safe form has the same name. Tracked as a known coverage hole; the few-shot library deliberately avoids these CVEs as exemplars.

regression_test_strategy.before_phase_6_5_merge: Run the new framework_spec generator against the same 88-CVE corpus on the same Qwen model. Gate the merge on validated_pct ≥ 50% (allow some headroom for the Semgrep-grammar drift) AND the per-language coverage matches Phase 5 within 10pp.
regression_test_strategy.ongoing: Capture the next tournament-final snapshot (this file's successor) the moment the new generator stabilises, so future M-phase rewrites have the same kind of frozen reference.
